_comment: HomeBridge Compliance Rule Library — Mountain/Western Region, Phase 2
_note: This file is the single source of truth for rule verification dates and citations. Update it when a compliance partner completes a review cycle. Do NOT edit the 'version' field by hand — it is managed by the /admin/compliance/verify-state endpoint.
_framework: Rules are organized under the 12-Layer Real Estate Content Compliance Framework (HomeBridge Compliance Addendum, April 18 2026). Every entry carries a 'layer' field referencing the governing layer. No rule is added without a primary source citation and a layer assignment.
scope: Mountain/Western Region (14 states) — CO live, others staged

4.name: State Commission Advertising Rules
4.authority: State real estate commission regulations — all jurisdictions

7.name: State Deceptive Trade Practices
7.authority: CO C.R.S. § 6-1-105 / TX DTPA / CA UCL and equivalents

8.name: Copyright / IP Law
8.authority: 17 U.S.C. / MLS listing data ownership rules

9.name: MLS Rules
9.authority: NAR MLS Policy Statement 7.58 / local MLS rules

10.name: Platform Ad Rules
10.authority: Meta Special Ad Categories / Google Ads Housing Policy / LinkedIn Campaign Policies

11.name: FinCEN
11.authority: Bank Secrecy Act / FinCEN GTOs / 2024 Proposed Rulemaking

12.name: AI-Generated Content Compliance
12.authority: FTC AI Guidance 2023-2024 / CA AB 2602 / NAR AI Guidelines

fair_housing_act.review_notes: Phrase lists grounded in HUD formal guidance, administrative case law, and federal court decisions. Master bedroom/bath explicitly cleared per Achtenberg 1995. Context-dependent language evaluated in semantic pass.
fair_housing_act.cadence: annual + triggered (review when HUD issues new enforcement guidance)

hud_advertising_guidance.review_notes: April 2024 guidance covers digital/social platforms explicitly. Ordinary reader standard codified. Next major review point: any new FHEO digital advertising guidance.

hud_regulations_part109.citation: HUD 24 C.F.R. Part 109 — Fair Housing Advertising Regulations (1989, operative)
hud_regulations_part109.review_notes: Part 109 was formally withdrawn in 1996 but remains operative enforcement guidance. The word/phrase lists derived from Part 109 continue to be applied in HUD enforcement proceedings.

achtenberg_memo.citation: Achtenberg Memo, January 9, 1995 — HUD FHEO Internal Enforcement Guidance
achtenberg_memo.review_notes: Explicitly clears 'master bedroom,' 'master bath,' 'desirable neighborhood,' and similar terms. This memo has not been superseded. Master bedroom is NOT flagged in Pass 1.
achtenberg_memo.cadence: annual — verify memo has not been superseded

nar_code_of_ethics.citation: NAR Code of Ethics Articles 10, 12, 15, 16 (2026 edition)
nar_code_of_ethics.review_notes: NAR updates the Code of Ethics each January. 2026 edition verified. Article 10 adds sexual orientation and gender identity beyond FHA protected classes. Article 12 requires truthful presentation. Article 15 prohibits false statements. Article 16 governs solicitation.
nar_code_of_ethics.cadence: annual — NAR releases updated edition each January

doj_steering.review_notes: Steering phrase list derived from DOJ enforcement patterns and HUD Part 109 guidance. Blockbusting phrases included per FHA § 3604(e).
doj_steering.cadence: annual + triggered (DOJ consent decree / new enforcement cases)

respa_section8.review_notes: Kickback and unearned fee prohibition. Phrase list targets explicit referral fee solicitations and co-marketing arrangements with lenders or settlement service providers.

regulation_z.review_notes: Trigger terms for mortgage advertising. Any mention of specific rates or terms triggers full disclosure requirements.

cfpb_udaap.review_notes: Unfair, deceptive, or abusive acts or practices. Applies to superlative claims, unsubstantiated performance claims, and deceptive testimonials in agent content.

ftc_endorsement_guides.citation: FTC Endorsement and Testimonial Guides — 16 C.F.R. Part 255 (revised 2023)
ftc_endorsement_guides.review_notes: Revised guides effective June 2023. Require clear disclosure of material connections in testimonials. 'Results not typical' and paid relationship disclosures. AI-generated content intersects with Layer 12.
ftc_endorsement_guides.cadence: semi-annual — FTC active on AI and endorsement guidance in 2024-2026

civil_rights_act.review_notes: Prohibits racial discrimination in property transactions including advertising. Substantial overlap with Layer 1 FHA coverage. Racial steering, race-based neighborhood descriptions, and racially coded language flagged under FHA rules also address this layer.

ftc_ai_guidance.citation: FTC AI Guidance 2023-2024 / CA AB 2602 / NAR AI Use Guidelines
ftc_ai_guidance.review_notes: CIR credential is HomeBridge's primary AI disclosure mechanism — licensed human reviewed and approved every piece of AI-generated content. AI authorship disclosure rules required in states with active AI disclosure laws. Review quarterly — this layer changes fastest.
ftc_ai_guidance.cadence: quarterly minimum — CA AB 2602, CO SB 205, and emerging state AI laws require close monitoring

fincen_gto.citation: Bank Secrecy Act / FinCEN Geographic Targeting Orders / 2024 Proposed Rulemaking
fincen_gto.review_notes: Low direct applicability to content generation. Monitor 2024 proposed FinCEN real estate AML rule for finalization. Content must avoid implying anonymity, promoting opaque ownership structures, or encouraging strategies that could be misinterpreted under AML rules.

CO.label: Colorado Real Estate Commission
CO.review_notes: Rule 6.10 governs advertising. Square footage disclosure requirement verified. Franchise legend requirement verified. CREC website reviewed April 2026 — no pending rule changes detected.
CO.cadence: quarterly — CO is the live production state

WY.label: Wyoming Real Estate Commission
WY.review_notes: Advertising statute verified April 2026. Wyoming has limited advertising rule specificity; general disclosure requirements apply.

MT.label: Montana Board of Realty Regulation
MT.review_notes: ARM 24.210.641 is the advertising rule. MCA § 37-51-321 is the underlying statutory authority. Verified April 2026.

ID.label: Idaho Real Estate Commission
ID.review_notes: Idaho Code § 54-2038 is the advertising statute. IREC Guideline 13 provides advertising specifics. Verified April 2026.

UT.label: Utah Division of Real Estate
UT.review_notes: R162-2f-401h governs advertising. Brokerage name disclosure requirements verified. Verified April 2026.

NM.label: New Mexico Real Estate Commission
NM.review_notes: NMAC 16.61.32.8 is the advertising rule. Verified April 2026.

TX.label: Texas Real Estate Commission (TREC)
TX.review_notes: §535.155 governs advertising; §535.154 covers team advertising. TREC rules frequently updated — check TREC website before each quarterly review. Verified April 2026.
TX.cadence: quarterly — TREC is an active rulemaking body

AZ.label: Arizona Department of Real Estate (ADRE)
AZ.review_notes: A.A.C. R4-28-502 updated December 13, 2025. Significant revision — verify against current ADRE rule text before each review. Verified April 2026.
AZ.cadence: quarterly — recent December 2025 rule revision warrants close monitoring

NV.label: Nevada Real Estate Division
NV.review_notes: NAC 645.610 is the advertising regulation. NRS 645.315 is the statutory authority. Verified April 2026.

OR.label: Oregon Real Estate Agency
OR.review_notes: OAR 863-015-0125 governs advertising. Oregon Real Estate Agency website reviewed April 2026.

WA.label: Washington Department of Licensing (DOL)
WA.review_notes: WAC 308-124B-210 governs advertising. Washington DOL online advertising guidelines also verified. Specialty claim substantiation requirement noted. Verified April 2026.

CA.label: California Department of Real Estate (DRE)
CA.review_notes: AB 1650 (eff. Jan. 1, 2018) eliminated the print ad license number exception. DRE RE 559 governs team name advertising. License number disclosure required on all first-point-of-contact materials. Verified April 2026.
CA.cadence: quarterly — California DRE is highly active; monitor for new bulletins

AK.label: Alaska Real Estate Commission
AK.review_notes: Best Practice advertising guidance verified. Brokerage name required in all advertising including social media. Written consent required before advertising property. Verified April 2026.

HI.label: Hawaii Real Estate Commission
HI.review_notes: HAR § 16-99-11 governs advertising comprehensively. Leasehold disclosure requirement is Hawaii-specific — any leasehold property must be identified as such. EHO logotype required in all advertising. Verified April 2026.
